Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting significant renal dysfunction, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease with impaired tubular function. Elevated alkaline phosphatase may indicate hepatic or bone involvement. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal pathology. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.
